# Learn App Summary — ACE-Test-002 (Focus-Group Facilitation Training)

> **Stub.** Used by skills downstream of `pdd-to-learn-app` (e.g., `app-test`, `training-materials`) to know the shape of the Learn app. The actual app is generated by Nova at run time from the PDD's Learn App Specification.
>
> **Archetype:** `focus-group`. This is a **facilitation training app**, not a form-walkthrough app. See `skills/pdd-to-learn-app/SKILL.md` `## Archetypes` → `focus-group` for the brief structure.

## App overview

- **Name:** Vaccine Hesitancy Focus Group Facilitation Training
- **Type:** Learn (CommCare training app)
- **Target user:** TestLand Health Partners community-engagement staff
- **Prerequisite:** None — this is the entry point
- **Completion gate:** Pass all 8 module knowledge checks before being marked session-ready

## Modules

| # | Module | Purpose | Knowledge check |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Facilitation basics | Opening sessions, ground rules, body language, time management | 3-question quiz |
| 2 | Probing techniques | "Tell me more," neutral redirect, follow-up patterns | 3-question quiz |
| 3 | Neutral framing | Asking sensitive questions without conveying judgment | 3-question quiz |
| 4 | Group dynamics | Managing dominant participants, drawing out quiet ones, handling disagreement | 3-question quiz |
| 5 | Question guide walkthrough | Reading the PDD's question guide aloud with rationale for ordering and probes | 3-question quiz |
| 6 | Session form walkthrough | Opening the Deliver app, completing per-domain summary, uploading audio + photo | 3-question quiz |
| 7 | Consent and ethics | Verbal consent script, audio recording consent, withdrawal handling | 3-question quiz |
| 8 | Logistics | Venue setup, attendance register, compensation distribution | 3-question quiz |

## Notable structural differences from atomic-visit Learn apps

- **No data-collection-form walkthrough.** The Deliver app exists but the training doesn't focus on "fill in this field then this field" — it focuses on the craft of facilitation, with the form walkthrough as one of 8 modules, not the whole app.
- **No skip-logic teaching.** Focus-group session forms have less branching than atomic-visit forms.
- **Knowledge checks are concept-based, not procedure-based.** ("Which of the following is a leading question?" not "What button do you press to save the form?")
